review_title: Product Foundation Independent Claude Code Review
review_scope: Independent Claude Code validation of the Product Foundation state after GL-075 (OpenCode/Kimi review gate) and GL-076 (Runtime Configuration Enforcement Baseline). Covers GL-075 conclusion validity, GL-076 implementation match, runtime configuration enforcement correctness, security and secret-exposure posture, API-first boundary compliance, implementation sequencing, test strategy adequacy, and GL-077 next-issue validation.

findings.summary: GL-075 conclusions remain accurate and applicable. The six cross-cutting risks identified by GL-075 are still relevant. The recommended GL-076 -> GL-077 -> GL-078 -> GL-079 ordering is being followed correctly. The final disposition of proceed_with_cautions remains appropriate. Nothing in GL-076 invalidates the GL-075 conclusions.
findings.recommendation: Accept GL-075 findings as valid. Continue following the recommended implementation sequence.

findings.summary: GL-076 implementation in backend/src/runtime_config.py matches the intended first implementation cut defined in docs/product_foundation_implementation_cut.md. The module is minimal (1 source file), stdlib-only (os and typing), fail-closed (ValueError on invalid modes), and secret-safe. Three public functions are sound: get_runtime_mode(), is_production_like(), describe_runtime_config(). File count is well within the 10-file limit. All deferred items (secret manager, auth, persistence, deployment wiring) are correctly left to future blocks.
findings.recommendation: Accept GL-076 implementation as a complete and correct first cut. No changes required.

findings.summary: GL-076 itself is secret-safe. describe_runtime_config() does not expose any environment variable values, tokens, keys, connection strings, or credentials. get_runtime_mode() error messages are safe. However, two persistent risks remain: (1) the demo Ed25519 keypair is still in the repository and is the primary stop gate before GL-079; (2) no log redaction exists yet, making GL-078 a prerequisite before any runtime configuration state is logged.
findings.recommendation: Enforce demo keypair removal as a hard stop gate before GL-079. Establish log redaction in GL-078 before any config-state logging is added.

findings.summary: GL-076 does not cross any extension boundary. runtime_config.py does not add or modify any API routes, OpenAPI schema, FastAPI routers, or domain modules. It is a pure utility leaf node with no side effects at import time. The dependency direction is correct: other modules will import it; it does not import them.
findings.recommendation: No boundary concerns. Continue following the established boundary discipline for GL-078+.

findings.summary: The GL-076 -> GL-077 -> GL-078 -> GL-079 ordering is correct and is being followed. GL-076 and GL-077 are both merged. GL-078 (Structured Logging / Correlation ID Helper Baseline) is the correct current next issue. GL-079 (Secret Source Boundary Hardening) must not start until GL-078 is merged and the demo keypair is removed. The sequence from the GL-075 review gate remains valid.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with GL-078 as the next issue. Enforce the GL-079 stop gate strictly.

findings.summary: The GL-076 test suite is adequate for a baseline. Three test classes cover mode detection, production-like classification, and safe metadata inspection. Security-focused tests in TestDescribeRuntimeConfig explicitly assert that sensitive terms do not appear in output — a positive pattern to carry forward. Gap: no integration-level tests connecting get_runtime_mode() to the startup path. This is acceptable for a baseline and should be added when GL-076 is wired into application startup.
findings.recommendation: Accept GL-076 test coverage as adequate. Carry the security-assertion pattern forward to GL-078 logging and GL-079 secret boundary tests.

findings.summary: GL-077 Health / Readiness Endpoint Baseline was the correct next issue after GL-076, as recommended by GL-075. GL-077 is already merged to main (commits 26e447f and a62c67a) at the time of this review. This confirms the implementation sequence was followed correctly. GL-078 (Structured Logging / Correlation ID Helper Baseline) is now the current next recommended issue.
findings.recommendation: GL-077 is complete. Proceed with GL-078 as the next issue.

risks.title: Demo Ed25519 keypair in repository
risks.description: The demo Ed25519 keypair remains in the repository. This is a hard stop gate before GL-079 (Secret Source Boundary Hardening).
risks.mitigation: Remove or supersede the demo keypair as part of GL-079. Enforce as a hard stop gate.

risks.title: No log redaction boundary
risks.description: No structured logging helper with secret redaction exists. Any future logging of runtime configuration state risks accidental secret exposure.
risks.mitigation: GL-078 must establish log redaction before any config-state logging is added to the codebase.

risks.title: Configuration drift for non-mode configuration categories
risks.description: GL-076 covers runtime mode detection. The design document defines 20 configuration categories requiring explicit validation in production mode. The remaining 19 categories have no enforcement layer yet.
risks.mitigation: Add configuration category validation incrementally across GL-078+ blocks. Document each new category as it is added.

risks.title: No PostgreSQL CI gating
risks.description: SQLite is still the default. PostgreSQL is not CI-gated. All persistence work (GL-080+) is blocked until PostgreSQL CI gating is established.
risks.mitigation: Add PostgreSQL CI gating before GL-080 starts.

risks.title: Premature production readiness claims
risks.description: All P0 gates remain open. GrantLayer is not production-ready. Documentation and communication must not imply production readiness.
risks.mitigation: Do not claim production readiness until all P0 gates close: PostgreSQL CI-gated, backup/restore, metrics/alerting, incident automation, API contract frozen, demo keypair removed, auth hardened.

recommended_next_issues.title: Health / Readiness Endpoint Baseline
recommended_next_issues.rationale: GL-077 was the correct next issue after GL-076. It is already merged, confirming the sequencing. Minimal /health and /readiness endpoints without DB/auth/metrics dependencies.
recommended_next_issues.stop_gate_for: Confirms runtime mode and configuration boundary is queryable before adding observability.

recommended_next_issues.title: Structured Logging / Correlation ID Helper Baseline
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Must be implemented before any config-state logging is added. Establishes log redaction boundary to prevent secret exposure. Correlation ID is required for distributed tracing groundwork.
recommended_next_issues.stop_gate_for: GL-079 Secret Source Boundary Hardening must not start until GL-078 is merged.

recommended_next_issues.title: Secret Source Boundary Hardening
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Removes the demo Ed25519 keypair and establishes the secret source boundary. Blocked on GL-078 (log redaction must exist before any secret-adjacent code is logged).
recommended_next_issues.stop_gate_for: GL-080 Persistence Backend Boundary Groundwork must not start until GL-079 is merged.

recommended_next_issues.title: Persistence Backend Boundary Groundwork
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Establishes PostgreSQL abstraction boundary and CI gating. Blocked on GL-079.

recommended_next_issues.title: Operator Access Hardening
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Wires operator role enforcement and token validation. Blocked on GL-075, GL-078, and GL-079 all merged.

stop_gates.trigger: Before GL-079 Secret Source Boundary Hardening
conditions: Demo Ed25519 keypair not present in test fixtures
Full backend suite passes with zero failures

stop_gates.trigger: Before GL-080 Persistence Backend Boundary Groundwork
conditions: Full backend suite passes with zero failures
Full backend suite passes with zero failures
Full backend suite passes with zero failures

disposition_summary: GL-075 conclusions remain valid. GL-076 implementation matches the intended first cut and is clean. GL-077 was the correct next issue (now confirmed by its merge). GL-078 is the current next recommended issue. Two high-severity risks persist: demo keypair in repository and no log redaction boundary. These are enforced as stop gates. GrantLayer is not production-ready.
review_notes: GL-075B is review-only. GL-075B adds no implementation.
GL-075B does not replace or revert GL-075.
GL-075B does not make GrantLayer production-ready.
Claude Code review should be used periodically, not as a required step for every issue.
